easy.title: Build a Complete Head Section
easy.description: Create an HTML page with a complete, well-organized head section that includes all essential meta tags: charset, viewport, description, author, robots, and a title. Make sure the tags are in the correct order.
easy.starterCode:
My Meta Tags Page

Check the head section for meta tags.
easy.solution:
My Meta Tags Page

This page has a complete set of essential meta tags in the correct order.

medium.title: SEO and Social Media Meta Tags
medium.description: Create an HTML page for a fictional blog post titled 'The Future of AI'. Include: proper charset and viewport, an SEO-optimized meta description (under 160 chars), robots meta tag, Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:type), Twitter Card tags (summary_large_image), and theme-color meta tag.
medium.starterCode:
The Future of AI

Blog post content goes here...
medium.solution:
The Future of AI

hard.title: Build a Meta Tag Validator Tool
hard.description: Create an HTML page that serves as a meta tag reference validator. The page should: display a table listing all major meta tags with their correct syntax, include a live demo of each meta tag type (charset, viewport, SEO, robots, Open Graph, Twitter, http-equiv, mobile), show simulated previews of how OG tags render on Facebook and Twitter Cards, and include a checklist for users to verify their own meta tags. All meta tags must be functional in the head of the document.
hard.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Meta Tag Validator & Reference</title>
    <!-- Add all meta tags you want to demonstrate here -->
    <style>
        body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; max-width: 1000px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 20px; }
        table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin: 20px 0; }
        th, td { border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 10px; text-align: left; }
        th { background: #333; color: white; }
        .card { border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; padding: 15px; margin: 15px 0; }
        .preview { max-width: 500px; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; overflow: hidden; }
        .good { color: green; }
        .bad { color: red; }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Build a comprehensive meta tag reference -->
</body>
</html>
hard.solution:
Meta Tag Validator & Reference

This page demonstrates all major meta tags. View the page source to see them in the head section.

Meta Tags Used on This Page

The following meta tags are active in this document's <head>:

TypeTagStatus
Charset<meta charset="UTF-8">Present
Viewport<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">Present
Description<meta name="description" content="...">Present (149 chars)
Robots<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">Present
Author<meta name="author" content="...">Present
Open Graph<meta property="og:title" content="...">Present
Twitter Card<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary">Present
Theme Color<meta name="theme-color" content="#333">Present
X-UA-Compatible<meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge">Present

Open Graph Preview (Facebook-style)

og:image Preview Area
metareference.com
Meta Tag Validator & Reference
Complete meta tag validator and reference guide for HTML developers.

Meta Tag Checklist

Use this checklist to verify your own pages:

  • <meta charset="UTF-8"> is the first element in <head>
  • <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"> is present
  • Meta description is under 160 characters and unique per page
  • Robots meta tag matches indexing intent (index/follow for public pages)
  • Open Graph tags include og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url
  • Twitter Card tags are present for Twitter sharing
  • Only one viewport meta tag exists
  • No deprecated or unnecessary meta tags (e.g., keywords)
  • Theme color matches brand if used
  • All meta tags are inside <head>, nowhere else
